A test case for verifying multiplication of string numbers in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the result of multiplying two string values, `"2"` and `"3"`, which are implicitly converted to numbers. The assertion verifies that the result of this operation is `6`. This demonstrates JavaScript's ability to perform type coercion, where the string values are automatically converted to numbers for the multiplication operation. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a single test case to illustrate this specific behavior of JavaScript.
